reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: The demo runs a REAL in-browser object-detection pass: worker.js imports the shared loadPipeline (lib/webai.js) and calls pipe(imageURL, {threshold:0.05, percentage:false}) on the object-detection pipeline (YOLOS-Tiny, a plain ViT-Tiny sequence detector), returning [{label, score, box}] in source pixels. No canned result. Grounded in the actual worker source + the immutable conformance suite, which tests `loads-clean` (console-clean, headless Chrome) and `calls-real-inference` (source pattern: pipeline(|createModelLoader|...). YOLOS treats detection as sequence prediction (no CNN backbone, no hand-tuned anchors); the ~30 MB q8 model is small enough to run per webcam frame. NOTE: this is a retrospective code+suite-grounded score — no fresh measured latency/screenshot was captured in this backfill pass, hence 4 not 5.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Web Worker (new Worker(worker.js, {type:'module'})); Runs once at a low floor and filters cached boxes via the slider; the webcam loop is self-pacing.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = COCO boxes. drawDetections() draws labelled boxes on <canvas id=stage aria-label='Detection result: image with labelled boxes'>; the See-inside table lists label/score/x/y/w/h and count chips summarise per-class counts.

At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/yolos-tiny, linked to its HF card), the real pipeline task (object-detection), backend (WASM — no WebGPU needed), quantisation (q8), download size (~30 MB) and license (Apache-2.0). The worker's loadPipeline call and the createModelLoader model config both use the SAME id/dtype/sizeMB — no invented API surface.

The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model:{modelId:'Xenova/yolos-tiny', runtime:'transformers.js', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:30, requiresWebGPU:false}) — no hand-rolled Load button. A valid current on-device model auto-initialises (checking → initialising → ready); only Download/Re-download/Update surface otherwise. Conformance tests `shared-auto-init-loader` (source: createModelLoader).
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on Xenova/yolos-tiny so the ~30 MB cache is tracked against the never-silently-re-download guarantee.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/inference state is announced in role=status aria-live regions (2 aria-live=polite regions in the page). Failures surface on the page as a readable `.status.err` message ("Detection failed: …"), never only in the console; the shared loader exposes Retry/re-download recovery. Runs on the WASM path everywhere — no WebGPU gate. Conformance tests `live-status-region`, `errors-on-the-page`.

Real keyboard-operable controls: file dropzone (role=button), sample thumbnails (role=button, Enter/Space), Detect button, Start/Stop webcam buttons (getUserMedia-gated), and a Score-threshold slider with an <output> mirror.

No canned/hardcoded output: every visible result is derived from the real worker run — boxes, counts and latency come straight from pipe() output. Conformance carries `no-fake-output` (manual-evidenced) and `calls-real-inference`.

Result canvas has a descriptive aria-label; sample thumbnails have alt text; buttons have names; status uses role=status aria-live. Conformance tests `images-have-alt` and `buttons-have-names` (both script assertions over the DOM).
rubric.notes: models.json records support.desktop/mobile = needs-review with an automated pre-scan flag of several sub-24px tap targets (sample thumbnails / small chips) 'agent to verify tap size' — a genuine open item, not yet manually confirmed.

rubric.evidence: Layout uses the shared design system (public/styles.css) with fluid flex/grid, flex-wrap and intrinsic sizing; conformance carries `responsive-desktop` and `responsive-mobile` (no-horizontal-overflow at ≈1280×800 and ≈360×740 DPR3 touch) plus `parity-both-classes`.
rubric.notes: support.desktop/mobile are still needs-review in models.json (awaiting an agent parity verdict); models.json records support.desktop/mobile = needs-review with an automated pre-scan flag of several sub-24px tap targets (sample thumbnails / small chips) 'agent to verify tap size' — a genuine open item, not yet manually confirmed.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Web Worker (new Worker(worker.js, {type:'module'})); The slider filters cached boxes; the webcam loop is self-pacing (busy guard). No main-thread inference or unbounded main-thread loop — INP stays low. Conformance carries `inference-off-main-thread` (source) and `inp-stays-low`.

guidanceConsulted.query: expose canvas content to assistive technology overlay boxes
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Give canvas-rendered content a meaningful accessible name and a text equivalent so assistive tech, translation and reading mode can reach it.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — labelled detection canvas + text See-inside table.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread heavy work in a web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inference in worker.js; slider filters cached boxes; webcam loop self-paces.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status role status aria-live; every control keyboard-operable with a name
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; keep all controls keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live, keyboard-operable dropzone/thumbnails, named buttons.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive layout no horizontal overflow fluid grid intrinsic sizing
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — fluid flex-wrap layout + full-width canvas.

openQuestions: No fresh measured latency/fps captured in this backfill; a headless run would let real-inference score 5.
Automated scan flags sub-24px tap targets on both classes — needs manual verification.

followUpGoals.goal: Capture a retained headless-Chrome run (real boxes + latency/fps) so real-inference can be evidenced at 5.
Verify sub-24px sample-thumbnail / chip tap targets and enlarge to ≥24px, then flip support to ok.

summary: Retrospective code+conformance-grounded critique for yolos-detection (Xenova/yolos-tiny, object-detection, q8, ~30 MB, Apache-2.0). Real ViT-Tiny sequence detector run off-thread; labelled canvas overlay, See-inside table, score slider filtering a cached run, live webcam loop, shared auto-init loader, live status + on-page errors. Honest gaps: no fresh measured latency captured here (real-inference 4) and sub-24px tap-target flags keeping support at needs-review (accessibility/responsive 4).
